name: no_axonendpoint_no_inference
source: flow F() -> Unit { step S { ask: "x" } }
comment: No axonendpoint to infer on; expected_implicit_transport=null means corpus skips the endpoint assertion. Sentinel entry for fixture-walker tests

name: disjunct_a_implicit_sse
source:
flow Live() -> Unit { step Stream { ask: "x" output: Stream<Token> } }
axonendpoint Live { method: POST path: "/live" execute: Live }
comment: Disjunct (a) — flow has a step with output: Stream<T>; axonendpoint omits transport → D1 fires. Rust parser does NOT yet accept Stream<T> as a step output_type (known gap tracked since Fase 30.e); the Rust drift gate skips this entry. Python carries the test on its own until the Rust frontend completion sub-fase lands

name: disjunct_b_implicit_sse_via_apply
comment: Disjunct (b) — apply: tool_name where tool has stream effect; Kivi case 2026-05-11

name: no_stream_no_implicit_sse
source:
flow Compute() -> Int { step Calc { ask: "x" output: Int } }
axonendpoint Compute { method: POST path: "/c" execute: Compute }
comment: Negative — no stream effects, no transport declared → json (D1 default)

name: declared_sse_wins
comment: Declared sse with stream effects — implicit equals declared

name: declared_sse_no_stream_compile_error_skip
source:
flow F() -> Unit { step S { ask: "x" } }
axonendpoint F { method: POST path: "/f" execute: F transport: sse }
comment: Declared sse without stream effects — Fase 30.c would reject at type-check; but the implicit_transport function itself returns the DECLARED value regardless (the rejection is a separate check)

name: declared_json_overrides_implicit
comment: D3 — explicit transport: json always wins, even when flow produces a stream

name: declared_ndjson_inferred_as_sse
comment: D2 — ndjson namespace reserved; inferred as sse for wire-format purposes today

name: missing_execute_flow_defaults_json
source: axonendpoint Orphan { method: POST path: "/o" execute: UnknownFlow }
comment: Endpoint references a flow that doesn't exist; inference defaults to json

name: multi_axonendpoint_same_flow_inference_consistent
comment: E1 omits transport → implicit sse; the sibling E2 with transport: json doesn't affect E1's inference

name: multi_axonendpoint_same_flow_explicit_opt_out
comment: Same source as previous entry; checking E2's explicit opt-out is honored

name: non_stream_flow_with_keepalive_doesnt_imply_sse
source:
flow F() -> Int { step S { ask: "x" output: Int } }
axonendpoint F { method: POST path: "/f" execute: F keepalive: 5s }
comment: keepalive declared but no stream effects + no transport → still json (keepalive alone doesn't trigger SSE inference; it's a configuration field, not a wire-format signal)

name: stream_effect_via_use_tool
comment: Disjunct (b) variant — use tool() flow-step (vs apply: in step body). Rust parser doesn't currently accept `use Tool()` at flow body level (Fase 30.e known gap). Python carries it; Rust skips

name: stream_effect_pause_upstream
comment: All 4 Fase 11.a backpressure policies (drop_oldest, degrade_quality, pause_upstream, fail) trigger inference

name: stream_effect_fail
comment: All 4 Fase 11.a backpressure policies trigger inference

name: stream_effect_degrade_quality
comment: All 4 Fase 11.a backpressure policies trigger inference

name: tool_with_non_stream_effect_no_implicit
comment: Tool has effects but not a stream: effect → no inference fires → json

name: tool_with_mixed_effects_stream_present
comment: Tool effects row contains stream: alongside other effects → inference fires

name: apply_ref_unknown_name_no_implicit
source:
flow F() -> Unit { step S { ask: "x" apply: ghost_tool } }
axonendpoint F { method: POST path: "/f" execute: F }
comment: apply: references a name that doesn't resolve to any tool → no inference fires (defensive)

name: stream_output_int_inner_type
source:
flow F() -> Unit { step S { ask: "x" output: Stream<Int> } }
axonendpoint F { method: POST path: "/f" execute: F }
comment: Stream<T> inner type is irrelevant — any T triggers disjunct (a). Rust gap same as disjunct_a_implicit_sse

name: stream_output_custom_type_inner
source:
flow F() -> Unit { step S { ask: "x" output: Stream<MyType> } }
axonendpoint F { method: POST path: "/f" execute: F }
comment: Stream<MyType> still triggers disjunct (a) even if MyType isn't otherwise defined. Rust gap same as above

name: non_stream_apply_with_explicit_json
source:
flow F() -> Int { step S { ask: "x" output: Int } }
axonendpoint F { method: POST path: "/f" execute: F transport: json }
comment: Non-stream flow + explicit transport: json — declared wins; no inference fires anyway

name: stream_field_inside_string_doesnt_count
source:
flow F() -> Unit { step S { ask: "the keyword stream:fail must not match" } }
axonendpoint F { method: POST path: "/f" execute: F }
comment: Inference uses AST-level predicates, not source-text substring — `stream:fail` inside a string literal must NOT trigger SSE inference (only the runtime defensive source-text bridge does that)

name: empty_flow_body_no_implicit
source:
flow F() -> Unit { }
axonendpoint F { method: POST path: "/f" execute: F }
comment: Empty flow body has no steps; no disjunct can fire; default json

name: axonendpoint_without_execute_field
source: axonendpoint Lonely { method: POST path: "/l" }
comment: Endpoint missing execute_flow entirely; inference can't even attempt; default json
